description_long: This obelisk features a broad, roughly circular face with a central protruding cylindrical element, giving it a unique and abstract appearance. The main body appears to be made of a stone-like material with a matte, slightly rough surface, and the central cylinder is of a different, possibly metallic or painted material. The edges of the obelisk are irregular and organic, lacking sharp corners or geometric precision, which adds to its sculptural quality. The central protrusion is prominent and extends outward, suggesting it may serve as a focal point or symbolic element. The coloration is primarily a mix of beige and light gray tones, with some subtle variations in shading. There are no visible inscriptions, ornamentation, or additional structural features such as steps or platforms at the base.
description: An abstract, circular obelisk with a central cylindrical protrusion, made of stone-like material with irregular edges and a matte, rough finish.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the obelisk from above, highlighting the broad, circular face and the central cylindrical protrusion surrounded by a sandy, textured surface.
description_view_1: View 1 presents a side profile, emphasizing the thinness of the main body and the outward extension of the central cylinder.
description_view_2: View 2 displays the obelisk from below or the opposite face, again showing the central protrusion and the irregular, organic edge.
description_view_3: View 3 offers another side profile, similar to View 1, but from the opposite direction, confirming the flatness and thinness of the main body.
attribute_base_design: The base is integrated into the irregular, organic shape of the main body, with no distinct platform, steps, or decorative features. It appears to rest flat on the ground.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is not pointed but rather features a central cylindrical protrusion extending outward from the center of the circular face, with a slightly rough finish and no unique decorations.
